<transformation>
The input is treated as a tile map. The output is NxM times larger than the input (where the input is N rows x M cols). Each input cell at (BR, BC) with a non-zero value causes a copy of the full input to be placed in the corresponding NxM block of the output (at rows BR*N .. BR*N+N-1, cols BC*M .. BC*M+M-1). Each input cell with value 0 produces an all-zero block at that position.
</transformation>

<asp>
% Count distinct row/col indices — equals grid dimensions for 0-indexed inputs
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(N) :- N = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Output is NR*NR rows and NC*NC cols (input tiled by itself)
output_cell(R, C) :-
    input(BR, BC, _), input(DR, DC, _),
    n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
    R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC.

1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Non-zero block: the sub-cell must equal the input value at the relative position
:- input(BR, BC, V), V != 0,
   input(DR, DC, TileColor),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != TileColor.

% Zero block: every sub-cell must be 0
:- input(BR, BC, 0),
   input(DR, DC, _),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != 0.
</asp>
</example_1>

<transformation>
The output is the same size as the input. Color-3 cells are unchanged. Non-3 cells (value 0) that are enclosed by color-3 walls — i.e., cannot reach the grid boundary via 4-connected movement through non-3 cells — are filled with color 4. All other non-3 cells remain 0.
</transformation>

<asp>
% Output has the same dimensions as the input
output_cell(R, C) :- input(R, C, _).
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Grid boundary coordinates
min_row(R) :- R = #min{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
max_row(R) :- R = #max{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
min_col(C) :- C = #min{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.
max_col(C) :- C = #max{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.

% 4-connected adjacency offsets
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Non-3 cells on the grid boundary are reachable from outside
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_row(Rmin), R = Rmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_row(Rmax), R = Rmax.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_col(Cmin), C = Cmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_col(Cmax), C = Cmax.

% Flood-fill outward through non-3 cells
outside(R2, C2) :-
    outside(R1, C1), delta(DR, DC),
    R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
    input(R2, C2, V), V != 3.

% Color-3 cells stay 3
:- input(R, C, 3), output(R, C, Color), Color != 3.

% Outside non-3 cells stay 0
:- outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 0.

% Interior non-3 cells (enclosed by 3s, not reachable from boundary) become 4
:- input(R, C, 0), not outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 4.
</asp>
</example_2>

% Determine dimensions
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R,_,_)}.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{C : input(_,C,_)}.

% Identify wall colors (colors that appear on outer boundary and are not 0)
wall_color(W) :- input(R,C,W), (R = 0; R = N-1; C = 0; C = M-1), W != 0.

% Non-wall predicate
non_wall(W) :- not wall_color(W).

% boundary non-wall cells (starting points for flood fill)
boundary_nonwall(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), non_wall(V), (R = 0; R = N-1; C = 0; C = M-1).

% Adjacency offsets
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Flood fill reachable cells (outside region)
reachable(R,C) :- boundary_nonwall(R,C).
reachable(R2,C2) :- reachable(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC), R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC, input(R2,C2,V), non_wall(V), reachable(R2,C2).

% Outside region: cell is reachable from a boundary non-wall cell via non-wall cells
outside(R,C) :- reachable(R,C).

% Define output facts and enforce correct colors
output_cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,V).   % just to have one per cell

color(0..9).  % automatically provided

% Exactly one output per cell
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

% Constraints for each cell
:- output(R,C,Color), input(R,C,V), outside(R,C), Color != V.
:- output(R,C,Color), not outside(R,C), input(R,C,V), V = 0, Color != 4.
:- output(R,C,Color), not outside(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 0, Color != V.

% Determine dimensions
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R,_,_)}.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{C : input(_,C,_)}.

% Wall colors
wall_color(W) :- input(R,C,W), (R = 0; R = N-1; C = 0; C = M-1), W != 0.

% Non-wall
non_wall(W) :- not wall_color(W).

% Starting points (boundary non-wall)
boundary_nonwall(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), not wall_color(V), (R = 0; R = N-1; C = 0; C = M-1).

% Flood fill (outside region)
outside(R,C) :- boundary_nonwall(R,C).
outside(R2,C2) :- outside(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC), R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC, input(R2,C2,V), non_wall(V), outside(R2,C2).

% Define a predicate for each cell
cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% Exactly one output per cell
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- cell(R,C).

% Constraints for correct output
:- output(R,C,Color), input(R,C,V), outside(R,C), Color != V.
:- output(R,C,Color), not outside(R,C), input(R,C,V), V = 0, Color != 4.
:- output(R,C,Color), not outside(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 0, Color != V.

% Adjacency offsets
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

refinements.response:
```asp
% Dimensions
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Wall colours – colours that appear on the outer border and are not 0
wall_color(W) :- input(R,C,W), (R = 0; R = N-1; C = 0; C = M-1), W != 0.

% Cells whose colour is not a wall colour
non_wall(W) :- not wall_color(W).

% Start cells for the flood‑fill: boundary cells whose colour is not a wall colour
boundary_nonwall(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), not wall_color(V), R = 0; R = N-1; C = 0; C = M-1.

% Cells reachable from a non‑wall boundary cell without crossing a wall
outside(R,C) :- boundary_nonwall(R,C).
outside(R2,C2) :- outside(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC), R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
                input(R2,C2,V), non_wall(V).

% One predicate for each cell (so the cardinality can enforce a unique colour)
cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% Exactly one output colour for every cell
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- cell(R,C).

% Enforce the correct colour according to the rule
% – cells that are “outside” must keep their original colour
:- output(R,C,Color), input(R,C,V), outside(R,C), Color != V.

% – interior zeros become 4
:- output(R,C,Color), not outside(R,C), input(R,C,V), V = 0, Color != 4.

% – interior non‑zero cells keep their colour
:- output(R,C,Color), not outside(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 0, Color != V.

% 4‑connected adjacency
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).
</asp>

% Dimensions
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Wall colours – colours that appear on the outer border and are not 0
wall_color(W) :- input(R,C,W), (R = 0; R = N-1; C = 0; C = M-1), W != 0.

% Cells whose colour is not a wall colour
non_wall(W) :- not wall_color(W).

% Start cells for the flood‑fill: boundary cells whose colour is not a wall colour
boundary_nonwall(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), not wall_color(V), R = 0; R = N-1; C = 0; C = M-1.

% Cells reachable from a non‑wall boundary cell without crossing a wall
outside(R,C) :- boundary_nonwall(R,C).
outside(R2,C2) :- outside(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC), R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
                input(R2,C2,V), non_wall(V).

% One predicate for each cell (so the cardinality can enforce a unique colour)
cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% Exactly one output colour for every cell
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- cell(R,C).

% Enforce the correct colour according to the rule
% – cells that are “outside” must keep their original colour
:- output(R,C,Color), input(R,C,V), outside(R,C), Color != V.

% – interior zeros become 4

<transformation>
The input is treated as a tile map. The output is NxM times larger than the input (where the input is N rows x M cols). Each input cell at (BR, BC) with a non-zero value causes a copy of the full input to be placed in the corresponding NxM block of the output (at rows BR*N .. BR*N+N-1, cols BC*M .. BC*M+M-1). Each input cell with value 0 produces an all-zero block at that position.
</transformation>

<asp>
% Count distinct row/col indices — equals grid dimensions for 0-indexed inputs
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(N) :- N = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Output is NR*NR rows and NC*NC cols (input tiled by itself)
output_cell(R, C) :-
    input(BR, BC, _), input(DR, DC, _),
    n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
    R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC.

1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Non-zero block: the sub-cell must equal the input value at the relative position
:- input(BR, BC, V), V != 0,
   input(DR, DC, TileColor),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != TileColor.

% Zero block: every sub-cell must be 0
:- input(BR, BC, 0),
   input(DR, DC, _),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != 0.
</asp>

<asp>
% Output has the same dimensions as the input
output_cell(R, C) :- input(R, C, _).
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Grid boundary coordinates
min_row(R) :- R = #min{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
max_row(R) :- R = #max{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
min_col(C) :- C = #min{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.
max_col(C) :- C = #max{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.

% 4-connected adjacency offsets
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Non-3 cells on the grid boundary are reachable from outside
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_row(Rmin), R = Rmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_row(Rmax), R = Rmax.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_col(Cmin), C = Cmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_col(Cmax), C = Cmax.

% Flood-fill outward through non-3 cells
outside(R2, C2) :-
    outside(R1, C1), delta(DR, DC),
    R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
    input(R2, C2, V), V != 3.

% Color-3 cells stay 3
:- input(R, C, 3), output(R, C, Color), Color != 3.

% Outside non-3 cells stay 0
:- outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 0.

% Interior non-3 cells (enclosed by 3s, not reachable from boundary) become 4
:- input(R, C, 0), not outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 4.
</asp>

n_cols(M) :- M = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Wall colours – colours that appear on the outer border and are not 0
wall_color(W) :- input(R,C,W), (R = 0; R = N-1; C = 0; C = M-1), W != 0.

% Cells whose colour is not a wall colour
non_wall(W) :- not wall_color(W).

% Start cells for the flood‑fill: boundary cells whose colour is not a wall colour
boundary_nonwall(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), not wall_color(V), R = 0; R = N-1; C = 0; C = M-1.

% Cells reachable from a non‑wall boundary cell without crossing a wall
outside(R,C) :- boundary_nonwall(R,C).
outside(R2,C2) :- outside(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC), R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
                input(R2,C2,V), non_wall(V).

% One predicate for each cell (so the cardinality can enforce a unique colour)
cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% Exactly one output colour for every cell
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- cell(R,C).

% Enforce the correct colour according to the rule
% – cells that are “outside” must keep their original colour
:- output(R,C,Color), input(R,C,V), outside(R,C), Color != V.

% – interior zeros become 4
:- output(R,C,Color), not outside(R,C), input(R,C,V), V = 0, Color != 4.

% – interior non‑zero cells keep their colour
:- output(R,C,Color), not outside(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 0, Color != V.

% 4‑connected adjacency
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).
</asp>

% Dimensions
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% wall colors: colors appearing on border and not 0
wall_color(W) :- input(R, C, W), R = 0, W != 0.
wall_color(W) :- input(R, C, W), R = N-1, W != 0.
wall_color(W) :- input(R, C, W), C = 0, W != 0.
wall_color(W) :- input(R, C, W), C = M-1, W != 0.

% non-wall: not wall_color
non_wall(W) :- not wall_color(W).

% boundary cells that are non-wall (starting points)
boundary_nonwall(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), not wall_color(V), R = 0.
boundary_nonwall(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), not wall_color(V), R = N-1.
boundary_nonwall(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), not wall_color(V), C = 0.
boundary_nonwall(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), not wall_color(V), C = M-1.

% 4-connectivity offsets
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% flood fill: outside cells reachable from a boundary_nonwall through non-wall cells
outside(R,C) :- boundary_nonwall(R,C).
outside(R2,C2) :- outside(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC), R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
               input(R2,C2,V), non_wall(V).

% interior zero cells: zero and not outside
interior_zero(R,C) :- not outside(R,C), input(R,C,0).

% cell predicate
cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% exactly one output per cell
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- cell(R,C).

% outside: preserve color
:- outside(R,C), input(R,C,V), output(R,C,Color), Color != V.

% interior non-zero: keep original
:- not outside(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 0, output(R,C,Color), Color != V.

% interior zero: must be 4
interior_zero(R,C) :- not outside(R,C), input(R,C,0).

:- interior_zero(R,C), output(R,C,Color), Color != 0.
:- interior_zero(R,C), output(R,C,Color), Color != 4.

% Dimensions
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Wall colors (appear on outer border and are not 0)
wall_color(W) :- input(R, C, W), R = 0, W != 0.
wall_color(W) :- input(R, C, W), R = N-1, W != 0.
wall_color(W) :- input(R, C, W), C = 0, W != 0.

% Cells that are not a wall colour
non_wall(W) :- not wall_color(W).

% Boundary cells that are not a wall colour
boundary_nonwall(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), not wall_color(V), R = 0.
boundary_nonwall(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), not wall_color(V), R = N-1.
boundary_nonwall(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), not wall_color(V), C = 0.
boundary_nonwall(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), not wall_color(V), C = M-1.

% 4‑connected adjacency offsets
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Flood‑fill: cells reachable from a non‑wall boundary cell without crossing a wall
outside(R,C) :- boundary_nonwall(R,C).
outside(R2,C2) :- outside(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC), R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
                input(R2,C2,V), non_wall(V).

% Cell predicate (used for cardinality)

% Exactly one output colour per cell

% Interior non‑zero cells keep original colour

% Interior zero cells become colour 4 (the only colour allowed)
interior_zero(R,C) :- not outside(R,C), input(R,C,0).
:- interior_zero(R,C), output(R,C,Color), Color != 4.

<asp>
% Determine grid dimensions
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Walls: any colour that appears on the outer border (row 0,
% row N‑1, column 0, column M‑1) and is not 0.
% The colour is stored in a unary predicate wall_color/1.
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
wall_color(Wc) :- input(R, C, Wc), R = 0,   Wc != 0.
wall_color(Wc) :- input(R, C, Wc), R = N-1, Wc != 0.
wall_color(Wc) :- input(R, C, Wc), C = 0,   Wc != 0.
wall_color(Wc) :- input(R, C, Wc), C = M-1, Wc != 0.

% Colours that are not walls
non_wall(Wc) :- not wall_color(Wc).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Start of the flood‑fill: boundary cells whose colour is not a wall.
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
boundary_nonwall(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), not wall_color(V), R = 0.
boundary_nonwall(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), not wall_color(V), R = N-1.
boundary_nonwall(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), not wall_color(V), C = 0.
boundary_nonwall(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), not wall_color(V), C = M-1.

% 4‑connected moves
delta(0, 1). delta(0, -1). delta(1, 0). delta(-1, 0).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Flood‑fill: cells reachable from a non‑wall boundary cell without
% crossing a wall colour.
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
outside(R, C) :- boundary_nonwall(R, C).
outside(R2, C2) :- outside(R1, C1), delta(DR, DC),
                R2 = R1 + DR, C2 = C1 + DC,
                input(R2, C2, V), non_wall(V).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Helper predicates
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
cell(R, C) :- input(R, C, _).

% Exactly one output colour per cell
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(C) } 1 :- cell(R, C).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% 1. Cells that are reachable from the boundary keep their original colour.
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
:- outside(R, C), input(R, C, V), output(R, C, Color), Color != V.

% 2. Interior non‑zero cells (not reachable) keep their original colour.
:- not outside(R, C), input(R, C, V), V != 0,
   output(R, C, Color), Color != V.

% 3. Interior zero cells become colour 4.
interior_zero(R, C) :- not outside(R, C), input(R, C, 0).
:- interior_zero(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 4.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% The transformation is now fully encoded.
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
</asp>
refinements.program:
% Determine grid dimensions
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Walls: any colour that appears on the outer border (row 0,
% row N‑1, column 0, column M‑1) and is not 0.
% The colour is stored in a unary predicate wall_color/1.
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
wall_color(Wc) :- input(R, C, Wc), R = 0,   Wc != 0.
wall_color(Wc) :- input(R, C, Wc), R = N-1, Wc != 0.
wall_color(Wc) :- input(R, C, Wc), C = 0,   Wc != 0.
wall_color(Wc) :- input(R, C, Wc), C = M-1, Wc != 0.

% Colours that are not walls
non_wall(Wc) :- not wall_color(Wc).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Start of the flood‑fill: boundary cells whose colour is not a wall.
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
boundary_nonwall(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), not wall_color(V), R = 0.
boundary_nonwall(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), not wall_color(V), R = N-1.
boundary_nonwall(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), not wall_color(V), C = 0.
boundary_nonwall(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), not wall_color(V), C = M-1.

% 4‑connected moves
delta(0, 1). delta(0, -1). delta(1, 0). delta(-1, 0).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Flood‑fill: cells reachable from a non‑wall boundary cell without
% crossing a wall colour.
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
outside(R, C) :- boundary_nonwall(R, C).
outside(R2, C2) :- outside(R1, C1), delta(DR, DC),
                R2 = R1 + DR, C2 = C1 + DC,
                input(R2, C2, V), non_wall(V).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Helper predicates
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
cell(R, C) :- input(R, C, _).

% Exactly one output colour per cell
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(C) } 1 :- cell(R, C).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% 1. Cells that are reachable from the boundary keep their original colour.
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
:- outside(R, C), input(R, C, V), output(R, C, Color), Color != V.

% 2. Interior non‑zero cells (not reachable) keep their original colour.
:- not outside(R, C), input(R, C, V), V != 0,
   output(R, C, Color), Color != V.

% 3. Interior zero cells become colour 4.
interior_zero(R, C) :- not outside(R, C), input(R, C, 0).
:- interior_zero(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 4.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% The transformation is now fully encoded.
% -----------------------------------------------------------------

<transformation>
The input is treated as a tile map. The output is NxM times larger than the input (where the input is N rows x M cols). Each input cell at (BR, BC) with a non-zero value causes a copy of the full input to be placed in the corresponding NxM block of the output (at rows BR*N .. BR*N+N-1, cols BC*M .. BC*M+M-1). Each input cell with value 0 produces an all-zero block at that position.
</transformation>

<asp>
% Count distinct row/col indices — equals grid dimensions for 0-indexed inputs
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(N) :- N = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Output is NR*NR rows and NC*NC cols (input tiled by itself)
output_cell(R, C) :-
    input(BR, BC, _), input(DR, DC, _),
    n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
    R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC.

1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Non-zero block: the sub-cell must equal the input value at the relative position
:- input(BR, BC, V), V != 0,
   input(DR, DC, TileColor),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != TileColor.

% Zero block: every sub-cell must be 0
:- input(BR, BC, 0),
   input(DR, DC, _),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != 0.
</asp>
</example_1>

<transformation>
The output is the same size as the input. Color-3 cells are unchanged. Non-3 cells (value 0) that are enclosed by color-3 walls — i.e., cannot reach the grid boundary via 4-connected movement through non-3 cells — are filled with color 4. All other non-3 cells remain 0.
</transformation>

<asp>
% Output has the same dimensions as the input
output_cell(R, C) :- input(R, C, _).
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Grid boundary coordinates
min_row(R) :- R = #min{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
max_row(R) :- R = #max{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
min_col(C) :- C = #min{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.
max_col(C) :- C = #max{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.

% 4-connected adjacency offsets
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Non-3 cells on the grid boundary are reachable from outside
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_row(Rmin), R = Rmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_row(Rmax), R = Rmax.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_col(Cmin), C = Cmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_col(Cmax), C = Cmax.

% Flood-fill outward through non-3 cells
outside(R2, C2) :-
    outside(R1, C1), delta(DR, DC),
    R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
    input(R2, C2, V), V != 3.

% Color-3 cells stay 3
:- input(R, C, 3), output(R, C, Color), Color != 3.

% Outside non-3 cells stay 0
:- outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 0.

% Interior non-3 cells (enclosed by 3s, not reachable from boundary) become 4
:- input(R, C, 0), not outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 4.
</asp>
</example_2>

<asp>
% Dimensions
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Wall colours – colours that appear on the outer border and are not 0
wall_color(W) :- input(R,C,W), (R = 0; R = N-1; C = 0; C = M-1), W != 0.

% Cells whose colour is not a wall colour
non_wall(W) :- not wall_color(W).

% Start cells for the flood‑fill: boundary cells whose colour is not a wall colour
boundary_nonwall(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), not wall_color(V), R = 0; R = N-1; C = 0; C = M-1.

% Cells reachable from a non‑wall boundary cell without crossing a wall
outside(R,C) :- boundary_nonwall(R,C).
outside(R2,C2) :- outside(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC), R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
                input(R2,C2,V), non_wall(V).

% One predicate for each cell (so the cardinality can enforce a unique colour)
cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% Exactly one output colour for every cell
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- cell(R,C).

% Enforce the correct colour according to the rule
% – cells that are “outside” must keep their original colour
:- output(R,C,Color), input(R,C,V), outside(R,C), Color != V.

% – interior zeros become 4
:- output(R,C,Color), not outside(R,C), input(R,C,V), V = 0, Color != 4.

% – interior non‑zero cells keep their colour
:- output(R,C,Color), not outside(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 0, Color != V.

% 4‑connected adjacency
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).
</asp>

<asp>
% Determine grid dimensions
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Walls: any colour that appears on the outer border (row 0,
% row N‑1, column 0, column M‑1) and is not 0.
% The colour is stored in a unary predicate wall_color/1.
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
wall_color(Wc) :- input(R, C, Wc), R = 0,   Wc != 0.
wall_color(Wc) :- input(R, C, Wc), R = N-1, Wc != 0.
wall_color(Wc) :- input(R, C, Wc), C = 0,   Wc != 0.
wall_color(Wc) :- input(R, C, Wc), C = M-1, Wc != 0.

% Colours that are not walls
non_wall(Wc) :- not wall_color(Wc).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Start of the flood‑fill: boundary cells whose colour is not a wall.
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
boundary_nonwall(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), not wall_color(V), R = 0.
boundary_nonwall(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), not wall_color(V), R = N-1.
boundary_nonwall(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), not wall_color(V), C = 0.
boundary_nonwall(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), not wall_color(V), C = M-1.

% 4‑connected moves
delta(0, 1). delta(0, -1). delta(1, 0). delta(-1, 0).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Flood‑fill: cells reachable from a non‑wall boundary cell without
% crossing a wall colour.
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
outside(R, C) :- boundary_nonwall(R, C).
outside(R2, C2) :- outside(R1, C1), delta(DR, DC),
                R2 = R1 + DR, C2 = C1 + DC,
                input(R2, C2, V), non_wall(V).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Helper predicates
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
cell(R, C) :- input(R, C, _).

% Exactly one output colour per cell
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(C) } 1 :- cell(R, C).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% 1. Cells that are reachable from the boundary keep their original colour.
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
:- outside(R, C), input(R, C, V), output(R, C, Color), Color != V.

% 2. Interior non‑zero cells (not reachable) keep their original colour.
:- not outside(R, C), input(R, C, V), V != 0,
   output(R, C, Color), Color != V.

% 3. Interior zero cells become colour 4.
interior_zero(R, C) :- not outside(R, C), input(R, C, 0).
:- interior_zero(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 4.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% The transformation is now fully encoded.
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
</asp>

% Determine dimensions
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Adjacent moves
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Border cells (grid edges)
border(R,C) :- R = 0.
border(R,C) :- R = N-1.
border(R,C) :- C = 0.
border(R,C) :- C = M-1.

% For each wall colour w in {3,4,6,2}
% w = 3
can_cross_3(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), V != 3.
reachable_3(R,C) :- border(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 3.
reachable_3(R2,C2) :- reachable_3(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC), R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
                     can_cross_3(R2,C2).
interior_3(R,C) :- not reachable_3(R,C).

% w = 4
can_cross_4(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), V != 4.
reachable_4(R,C) :- border(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 4.
reachable_4(R2,C2) :- reachable_4(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC), R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
                     can_cross_4(R2,C2).
interior_4(R,C) :- not reachable_4(R,C).

% w = 6
can_cross_6(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), V != 6.
reachable_6(R,C) :- border(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 6.
reachable_6(R2,C2) :- reachable_6(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC), R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
                     can_cross_6(R2,C2).
interior_6(R,C) :- not reachable_6(R,C).

% w = 2
can_cross_2(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), V != 2.
reachable_2(R,C) :- border(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 2.
reachable_2(R2,C2) :- reachable_2(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC), R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
                     can_cross_2(R2,C2).
interior_2(R,C) :- not reachable_2(R,C).

% Exactly one output colour per cell
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(C) } 1 :- input(R,C,_).

% Non‑zero cells keep their colour
:- input(R,C,V), V != 0, output(R,C,Color), Color != V.

% Zero cells interior to the innermost wall become the next colour
% 0 inside region bounded by 3 (but not by 4) => 4
:- input(R,C,0), interior_3(R,C), not interior_4(R,C), output(R,C,Color), Color != 4.

% 0 inside region bounded by 4 (but not by 6) => 6
:- input(R,C,0), interior_4(R,C), not interior_6(R,C), output(R,C,Color), Color != 6.

% 0 inside region bounded by 6 (but not by 2) => 2
:- input(R,C,0), interior_6(R,C), not interior_2(R,C), output(R,C,Color), Color != 2.

% 0 inside region bounded by 2 => 2
:- input(R,C,0), interior_2(R,C), output(R,C,Color), Color != 2.

```asp
% Determine dimensions
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% adjacency offsets
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% border cells
border(R,C) :- R = 0.
border(R,C) :- R = N-1.
border(R,C) :- C = 0.
border(R,C) :- C = M-1.

% Wall 3
can_cross_3(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), V != 3.
reachable_3(R,C) :- border(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 3.
reachable_3(R2,C2) :- reachable_3(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC), R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
                     can_cross_3(R2,C2).
interior_3(R,C) :- not reachable_3(R,C).

% Wall 4
can_cross_4(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), V != 4.
reachable_4(R,C) :- border(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 4.
reachable_4(R2,C2) :- reachable_4(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC), R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
                     can_cross_4(R2,C2).
interior_4(R,C) :- not reachable_4(R,C).

% Wall 6
can_cross_6(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), V != 6.
reachable_6(R,C) :- border(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 6.
reachable_6(R2,C2) :- reachable_6(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC), R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
                     can_cross_6(R2,C2).
interior_6(R,C) :- not reachable_6(R,C).

% Wall 2
can_cross_2(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), V != 2.
reachable_2(R,C) :- border(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 2.
reachable_2(R2,C2) :- reachable_2(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC), R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
                     can_cross_2(R2,C2).
interior_2(R,C) :- not reachable_2(R,C).

% Exactly one output colour per cell
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(C) } 1 :- input(R,C,_).

% Non‑zero cells keep their colour
:- input(R,C,V), V != 0, output(R,C,Color), Color != V.

% Zero cells interior to the innermost wall become the next colour
% 0 inside region bounded by 3 (but not by 4) => 4
:- input(R,C,0), interior_3(R,C), not interior_4(R,C), output(R,C,Color), Color != 4.

% 0 inside region bounded by 4 (but not by 6) => 6
:- input(R,C,0), interior_4(R,C), not interior_6(R,C), output(R,C,Color), Color != 6.

% 0 inside region bounded by 6 (but not by 2) => 2
:- input(R,C,0), interior_6(R,C), not interior_2(R,C), output(R,C,Color), Color != 2.

% 0 inside region bounded by 2 => 2
:- input(R,C,0), interior_2(R,C), output(R,C,Color), Color != 2.

<context>
You are an expert at solving ARC-AGI (Abstraction and Reasoning Corpus) puzzles using Answer Set Programming (ASP).

ARC-AGI puzzles show input-output grid transformations. Each cell contains a color value (integer 0–9). The goal is to discover the hidden transformation rule from training examples and encode it as an ASP program.
</context>

<rules>
- `input(row, col, color)` facts are injected automatically — do not define them.
- `color(0..9)` facts are injected automatically — do not define them.
- the output predicate is always `output(row, col, color)` — do not redefine it.
- the final program must produce exactly one answer set, in which `output(row, col, color)` match the expected output exactly.
</rules>

<transformation>
The input is treated as a tile map. The output is NxM times larger than the input (where the input is N rows x M cols). Each input cell at (BR, BC) with a non-zero value causes a copy of the full input to be placed in the corresponding NxM block of the output (at rows BR*N .. BR*N+N-1, cols BC*M .. BC*M+M-1). Each input cell with value 0 produces an all-zero block at that position.
</transformation>

<asp>
% Count distinct row/col indices — equals grid dimensions for 0-indexed inputs
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(N) :- N = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Output is NR*NR rows and NC*NC cols (input tiled by itself)
output_cell(R, C) :-
    input(BR, BC, _), input(DR, DC, _),
    n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
    R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC.

1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Non-zero block: the sub-cell must equal the input value at the relative position
:- input(BR, BC, V), V != 0,
   input(DR, DC, TileColor),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != TileColor.

% Zero block: every sub-cell must be 0
:- input(BR, BC, 0),
   input(DR, DC, _),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != 0.
</asp>
</example_1>

<transformation>
The output is the same size as the input. Color-3 cells are unchanged. Non-3 cells (value 0) that are enclosed by color-3 walls — i.e., cannot reach the grid boundary via 4-connected movement through non-3 cells — are filled with color 4. All other non-3 cells remain 0.
</transformation>

<asp>
% Output has the same dimensions as the input
output_cell(R, C) :- input(R, C, _).
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Grid boundary coordinates
min_row(R) :- R = #min{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
max_row(R) :- R = #max{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
min_col(C) :- C = #min{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.
max_col(C) :- C = #max{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.

% 4-connected adjacency offsets
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Non-3 cells on the grid boundary are reachable from outside
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_row(Rmin), R = Rmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_row(Rmax), R = Rmax.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_col(Cmin), C = Cmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_col(Cmax), C = Cmax.

% Flood-fill outward through non-3 cells
outside(R2, C2) :-
    outside(R1, C1), delta(DR, DC),
    R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
    input(R2, C2, V), V != 3.

% Color-3 cells stay 3
:- input(R, C, 3), output(R, C, Color), Color != 3.

% Outside non-3 cells stay 0
:- outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 0.

% Interior non-3 cells (enclosed by 3s, not reachable from boundary) become 4
:- input(R, C, 0), not outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 4.
</asp>

n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Wall colours – colours that appear on the outer border and are not 0
wall_color(W) :- input(R,C,W), (R = 0; R = N-1; C = 0; C = M-1), W != 0.

% Cells whose colour is not a wall colour

% Start cells for the flood‑fill: boundary cells whose colour is not a wall colour

% Cells reachable from a non‑wall boundary cell without crossing a wall

outside(R2,C2) :- outside(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC), R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
                input(R2,C2,V), non_wall(V).

% One predicate for each cell (so the cardinality can enforce a unique colour)
cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% Exactly one output colour for every cell
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- cell(R,C).

% Enforce the correct colour according to the rule
% – cells that are “outside” must keep their original colour

% – interior non‑zero cells keep their colour
:- output(R,C,Color), not outside(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 0, Color != V.

% 4‑connected adjacency
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

<asp>
% Determine grid dimensions
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Walls: any colour that appears on the outer border (row 0,
% row N‑1, column 0, column M‑1) and is not 0.
% The colour is stored in a unary predicate wall_color/1.
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
wall_color(Wc) :- input(R, C, Wc), R = 0,   Wc != 0.
wall_color(Wc) :- input(R, C, Wc), R = N-1, Wc != 0.
wall_color(Wc) :- input(R, C, Wc), C = 0,   Wc != 0.
wall_color(Wc) :- input(R, C, Wc), C = M-1, Wc != 0.

% Colours that are not walls
non_wall(Wc) :- not wall_color(Wc).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Start of the flood‑fill: boundary cells whose colour is not a wall.
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
boundary_nonwall(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), not wall_color(V), R = 0.
boundary_nonwall(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), not wall_color(V), R = N-1.
boundary_nonwall(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), not wall_color(V), C = 0.
boundary_nonwall(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), not wall_color(V), C = M-1.

% 4‑connected moves
delta(0, 1). delta(0, -1). delta(1, 0). delta(-1, 0).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Flood‑fill: cells reachable from a non‑wall boundary cell without
% crossing a wall colour.
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
outside(R, C) :- boundary_nonwall(R, C).
outside(R2, C2) :- outside(R1, C1), delta(DR, DC),
                R2 = R1 + DR, C2 = C1 + DC,
                input(R2, C2, V), non_wall(V).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Helper predicates
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
cell(R, C) :- input(R, C, _).

% Exactly one output colour per cell
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(C) } 1 :- cell(R, C).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% 1. Cells that are reachable from the boundary keep their original colour.
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
:- outside(R, C), input(R, C, V), output(R, C, Color), Color != V.

% 2. Interior non‑zero cells (not reachable) keep their original colour.
:- not outside(R, C), input(R, C, V), V != 0,
   output(R, C, Color), Color != V.

% 3. Interior zero cells become colour 4.
interior_zero(R, C) :- not outside(R, C), input(R, C, 0).
:- interior_zero(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 4.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% The transformation is now fully encoded.
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
</asp>

<asp>
% ---------- dimensions ----------
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% ---------- 4‑connected moves ----------
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% ---------- border cells ----------
border(R,C) :- R = 0.
border(R,C) :- R = N-1.
border(R,C) :- C = 0.
border(R,C) :- C = M-1.

% ---------- wall 3 ----------
outside_3(R,C) :- border(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 3.
outside_3(R2,C2) :- outside_3(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC), R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
                    input(R2,C2,V), V != 3.
interior_3(R,C) :- not outside_3(R,C).

% ---------- wall 4 ----------
outside_4(R,C) :- border(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 4.
outside_4(R2,C2) :- outside_4(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC), R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
                    input(R2,C2,V), V != 4.
interior_4(R,C) :- not outside_4(R,C).

% ---------- wall 6 ----------
outside_6(R,C) :- border(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 6.
outside_6(R2,C2) :- outside_6(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC), R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
                    input(R2,C2,V), V != 6.
interior_6(R,C) :- not outside_6(R,C).

% ---------- wall 2 ----------
outside_2(R,C) :- border(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 2.
outside_2(R2,C2) :- outside_2(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC), R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
                    input(R2,C2,V), V != 2.
interior_2(R,C) :- not outside_2(R,C).

% ---------- exactly one output colour per cell ----------
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(C) } 1 :- input(R,C,_).

% ---------- non‑zero cells keep their colour ----------
:- input(R,C,V), V != 0, output(R,C,Color), Color != V.

% ---------- interior zeros become the next colour ----------
% 0 inside region bounded by 3 (but not by 4) => 4
:- input(R,C,0), interior_3(R,C), not interior_4(R,C),
   output(R,C,Color), Color != 4.

% 0 inside region bounded by 4 (but not by 6) => 6
:- input(R,C,0), interior_4(R,C), not interior_6(R,C),
   output(R,C,Color), Color != 6.

% 0 inside region bounded by 6 (but not by 2) => 2
:- input(R,C,0), interior_6(R,C), not interior_2(R,C),
   output(R,C,Color), Color != 2.

% 0 inside region bounded by 2 => 2
:- input(R,C,0), interior_2(R,C), output(R,C,Color), Color != 2.
</asp>

We will have:

% Determine grid dimensions
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% 4‑connected moves
delta(0, 1). delta(0, -1). delta(1, 0). delta(-1, 0).

% Border cells
border(R, C) :- R = 0.
border(R, C) :- R = N-1.
border(R, C) :- C = 0.
border(R, C) :- C = M-1.

% Flood‑fill for wall colour 3
outside_3(R, C) :- border(R, C), input(R, C, V), V != 3.
outside_3(R2, C2) :- outside_3(R1, C1), delta(DR, DC),
    R2 = R1 + DR, C2 = C1 + DC,
    input(R2, C2, V), V != 3.

% Flood‑fill for wall colour 4
outside_4(R, C) :- border(R, C), input(R, C, V), V != 4.
outside_4(R2, C2) :- outside_4(R1, C1), delta(DR, DC),
    R2 = R1 + DR, C2 = C1 + DC,
    input(R2, C2, V), V != 4.

% Flood‑fill for wall colour 6
outside_6(R, C) :- border(R, C), input(R, C, V), V != 6.
outside_6(R2, C2) :- outside_6(R1, C1), delta(DR, DC),
    R2 = R1 + DR, C2 = C1 + DC,
    input(R2, C2, V), V != 6.

% Flood‑fill for wall colour 2
outside_2(R, C) :- border(R, C), input(R, C, V), V != 2.
outside_2(R2, C2) :- outside_2(R1, C1), delta(DR, DC),
    R2 = R1 + DR, C2 = C1 + DC,
    input(R2, C2, V), V != 2.

% Output for zero cells inside wall 3 (become 4)
output(R, C, 4) :-

% Count distinct row/col indices — equals grid dimensions for 0-indexed inputs
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Output is NR*NR rows and NC*NC cols (input tiled by itself)
output_cell(R, C) :-
    input(BR, BC, _), input(DR, DC, _),
    n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
    R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC.

1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(C) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Non-zero block: the sub-cell must equal the input value at the relative position
:- input(BR, BC, V), V != 0,
   input(DR, DC, TileColor),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != TileColor.

% Zero block: every sub-cell must be 0
:- input(BR, BC, 0),
   input(DR, DC, _),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != 0.

% Determine dimensions
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% 4‑connected moves
delta(0, 1). delta(0, -1). delta(1, 0). delta(-1, 0).

% Border cells
border(R, C) :- R = 0.
border(R, C) :- R = N-1.
border(R, C) :- C = 0.
border(R, C) :- C = M-1.

% Flood‑fill for wall colour 3 (cells that can escape without crossing 3)
outside_3(R, C) :- border(R, C), input(R, C, V), V != 3.
outside_3(R2, C2) :- outside_3(R1, C1), delta(DR, DC),
                     R2 = R1 + DR, C2 = C1 + DC,

% Determine number of rows and columns (0-indexed)
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% 4‑connected moves
delta(0, 1). delta(0, -1). delta(1, 0). delta(-1, 0).

% Border cells (grid edges)
border(R, C) :- R = 0.
border(R, C) :- R = N-1.
border(R, C) :- C = 0.
border(R, C) :- C = M-1.

% Flood‑fill for wall colour 3 (cells that can reach the border without crossing a 3)
outside_3(R, C) :- border(R, C), input(R, C, V), V != 3.
outside_3(R2, C2) :- outside_3(R1, C1), delta(DR, DC),
                     R2 = R1 + DR, C2 = C1 + DC,
                     input(R2, C2, V), V != 3.

% Flood‑fill for wall colour 4
outside_4(R, C) :- border(R, C), input(R, C, V), V != 4.
outside_4(R2, C2) :- outside_4(R1, C1), delta(DR, DC),
                     R2 = R1 + DR, C2 = C1 + DC,
                     input(R2, C2, V), V != 4.

% Flood‑fill for wall colour 6
outside_6(R, C) :- border(R, C), input(R, C, V), V != 6.
outside_6(R2, C2) :- outside_6(R1, C1), delta(DR, DC),
                     R2 = R1 + DR, C2 = C1 + DC,
                     input(R2, C2, V), V != 6.

% Flood‑fill for wall colour 2
outside_2(R, C) :- border(R, C), input(R, C, V), V != 2.
outside_2(R2, C2) :- outside_2(R1, C1), delta(DR, DC),
                     R2 = R1 + DR, C2 = C1 + DC,
                     input(R2, C2, V), V != 2.

% Determine the colour for zero cells, following the hierarchy 3→4→6→2→8→0
output(R, C, 4) :-
    input(R, C, 0),
    not outside_3(R, C).

output(R, C, 6) :-
    input(R, C, 0),
    outside_3(R, C),
    not outside_4(R, C).

output(R, C, 2) :-
    input(R, C, 0),
    outside_3(R, C),
    outside_4(R, C),
    not outside_6(R, C).

output(R, C, 8) :-
    input(R, C, 0),
    outside_3(R, C),
    outside_4(R, C),
    outside_6(R, C),
    not outside_2(R, C).

output(R, C, 0) :-
    input(R, C, 0),
    outside_3(R, C),
    outside_4(R, C),
    outside_6(R, C),
    outside_2(R, C).

% Non‑zero cells keep their original colour
output(R, C, V) :-
    input(R, C, V),
    V != 0.

% (No further nondeterminism – each cell has exactly one output)
% End of program
</asp>

% Output has the same dimensions as the input
output_cell(R, C) :- input(R, C, _).
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(C) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Grid boundary coordinates
min_row(R) :- R = #min{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
max_row(R) :- R = #max{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
min_col(C) :- C = #min{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.
max_col(C) :- C = #max{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.

% 4‑connected adjacency offsets
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Non-3 cells on the grid boundary are reachable from outside
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_row(Rmin), R = Rmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_row(Rmax), R = Rmax.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_col(Cmin), C = Cmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_col(Cmax), C = Cmax.

% Flood‑fill outward through non-3 cells
outside(R2, C2) :-
    outside(R1, C1), delta(DR, DC),
    R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
    input(R2, C2, V), V != 3.

% Color-3 cells stay 3
:- input(R, C, 3), output(R, C, Color), Color != 3.

% Outside non-3 cells stay 0
:- outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 0.

% Interior non-3 cells (enclosed by 3s, not reachable from boundary) become 4
:- input(R, C, 0), not outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 4.

% Zero block: every sub-cell must be 0
</asp>

refinements.prompt:
<context>
You are an expert at solving ARC-AGI (Abstraction and Reasoning Corpus) puzzles using Answer Set Programming (ASP).

ARC-AGI puzzles show input-output grid transformations. Each cell contains a color value (integer 0–9). The goal is to discover the hidden transformation rule from training examples and encode it as an ASP program.
</context>

<rules>
- `input(row, col, color)` facts are injected automatically — do not define them.
- `color(0..9)` facts are injected automatically — do not define them.
- the output predicate is always `output(row, col, color)` — do not redefine it.
- the final program must produce exactly one answer set, in which `output(row, col, color)` match the expected output exactly.
</rules>

Below are two fully worked examples.

<transformation>
The input is treated as a tile map. The output is NxM times larger than the input (where the input is N rows x M cols). Each input cell at (BR, BC) with a non-zero value causes a copy of the full input to be placed in the corresponding NxM block of the output (at rows BR*N .. BR*N+N-1, cols BC*M .. BC*M+M-1). Each input cell with value 0 produces an all-zero block at that position.
</transformation>

<asp>
% Count distinct row/col indices — equals grid dimensions for 0-indexed inputs
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(N) :- N = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Output is NR*NR rows and NC*NC cols (input tiled by itself)
output_cell(R, C) :-
    input(BR, BC, _), input(DR, DC, _),
    n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
    R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC.

1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Non-zero block: the sub-cell must equal the input value at the relative position
:- input(BR, BC, V), V != 0,
   input(DR, DC, TileColor),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != TileColor.

% Zero block: every sub-cell must be 0
:- input(BR, BC, 0),
   input(DR, DC, _),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != 0.
</asp>
</example_1>

<transformation>
The output is the same size as the input. Color-3 cells are unchanged. Non-3 cells (value 0) that are enclosed by color-3 walls — i.e., cannot reach the grid boundary via 4-connected movement through non-3 cells — are filled with color 4. All other non-3 cells remain 0.
</transformation>

<asp>
% Output has the same dimensions as the input
output_cell(R, C) :- input(R, C, _).
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Grid boundary coordinates
min_row(R) :- R = #min{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
max_row(R) :- R = #max{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
min_col(C) :- C = #min{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.
max_col(C) :- C = #max{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.

% 4-connected adjacency offsets
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Non-3 cells on the grid boundary are reachable from outside
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_row(Rmin), R = Rmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_row(Rmax), R = Rmax.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_col(Cmin), C = Cmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_col(Cmax), C = Cmax.

% Flood-fill outward through non-3 cells
outside(R2, C2) :-
    outside(R1, C1), delta(DR, DC),
    R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
    input(R2, C2, V), V != 3.

% Color-3 cells stay 3
:- input(R, C, 3), output(R, C, Color), Color != 3.

% Outside non-3 cells stay 0
:- outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 0.

% Interior non-3 cells (enclosed by 3s, not reachable from boundary) become 4
:- input(R, C, 0), not outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 4.
</asp>
</example_2>

<asp>
% Dimensions
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Wall colours – colours that appear on the outer border and are not 0
wall_color(W) :- input(R,C,W), (R = 0; R = N-1; C = 0; C = M-1), W != 0.

% Cells whose colour is not a wall colour
non_wall(W) :- not wall_color(W).

% Start cells for the flood‑fill: boundary cells whose colour is not a wall colour
boundary_nonwall(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), not wall_color(V), R = 0; R = N-1; C = 0; C = M-1.

% Cells reachable from a non‑wall boundary cell without crossing a wall
outside(R,C) :- boundary_nonwall(R,C).
outside(R2,C2) :- outside(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC), R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
                input(R2,C2,V), non_wall(V).

% One predicate for each cell (so the cardinality can enforce a unique colour)
cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% Exactly one output colour for every cell
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- cell(R,C).

% Enforce the correct colour according to the rule
% – cells that are “outside” must keep their original colour
:- output(R,C,Color), input(R,C,V), outside(R,C), Color != V.

% – interior zeros become 4
:- output(R,C,Color), not outside(R,C), input(R,C,V), V = 0, Color != 4.

% – interior non‑zero cells keep their colour
:- output(R,C,Color), not outside(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 0, Color != V.

% 4‑connected adjacency
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).
</asp>

<asp>
% Determine grid dimensions
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Walls: any colour that appears on the outer border (row 0,
% row N‑1, column 0, column M‑1) and is not 0.
% The colour is stored in a unary predicate wall_color/1.
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
wall_color(Wc) :- input(R, C, Wc), R = 0,   Wc != 0.
wall_color(Wc) :- input(R, C, Wc), R = N-1, Wc != 0.
wall_color(Wc) :- input(R, C, Wc), C = 0,   Wc != 0.
wall_color(Wc) :- input(R, C, Wc), C = M-1, Wc != 0.

% Colours that are not walls
non_wall(Wc) :- not wall_color(Wc).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Start of the flood‑fill: boundary cells whose colour is not a wall.
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
boundary_nonwall(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), not wall_color(V), R = 0.
boundary_nonwall(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), not wall_color(V), R = N-1.
boundary_nonwall(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), not wall_color(V), C = 0.
boundary_nonwall(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), not wall_color(V), C = M-1.

% 4‑connected moves
delta(0, 1). delta(0, -1). delta(1, 0). delta(-1, 0).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Flood‑fill: cells reachable from a non‑wall boundary cell without
% crossing a wall colour.
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
outside(R, C) :- boundary_nonwall(R, C).
outside(R2, C2) :- outside(R1, C1), delta(DR, DC),
                R2 = R1 + DR, C2 = C1 + DC,
                input(R2, C2, V), non_wall(V).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Helper predicates
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
cell(R, C) :- input(R, C, _).

% Exactly one output colour per cell
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(C) } 1 :- cell(R, C).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% 1. Cells that are reachable from the boundary keep their original colour.
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
:- outside(R, C), input(R, C, V), output(R, C, Color), Color != V.

% 2. Interior non‑zero cells (not reachable) keep their original colour.
:- not outside(R, C), input(R, C, V), V != 0,
   output(R, C, Color), Color != V.

% 3. Interior zero cells become colour 4.
interior_zero(R, C) :- not outside(R, C), input(R, C, 0).
:- interior_zero(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 4.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% The transformation is now fully encoded.
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
</asp>

% ---------- dimensions ----------
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% ---------- 4‑connected moves ----------
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% ---------- border cells ----------
border(R,C) :- R = 0.
border(R,C) :- R = N-1.
border(R,C) :- C = 0.
border(R,C) :- C = M-1.

% ---------- wall 3 ----------
outside_3(R,C) :- border(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 3.
outside_3(R2,C2) :- outside_3(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC), R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
                    input(R2,C2,V), V != 3.
interior_3(R,C) :- not outside_3(R,C).

% ---------- wall 4 ----------
outside_4(R,C) :- border(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 4.
outside_4(R2,C2) :- outside_4(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC), R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
                    input(R2,C2,V), V != 4.
interior_4(R,C) :- not outside_4(R,C).

% ---------- wall 6 ----------
outside_6(R,C) :- border(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 6.
outside_6(R2,C2) :- outside_6(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC), R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
                    input(R2,C2,V), V != 6.
interior_6(R,C) :- not outside_6(R,C).

% ---------- wall 2 ----------
outside_2(R,C) :- border(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 2.
outside_2(R2,C2) :- outside_2(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC), R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
                    input(R2,C2,V), V != 2.
interior_2(R,C) :- not outside_2(R,C).

% ---------- exactly one output colour per cell ----------
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(C) } 1 :- input(R,C,_).

% ---------- non‑zero cells keep their colour ----------
:- input(R,C,V), V != 0, output(R,C,Color), Color != V.

% ---------- interior zeros become the next colour ----------
% 0 inside region bounded by 3 (but not by 4) => 4
:- input(R,C,0), interior_3(R,C), not interior_4(R,C),
   output(R,C,Color), Color != 4.

% 0 inside region bounded by 4 (but not by 6) => 6
:- input(R,C,0), interior_4(R,C), not interior_6(R,C),
   output(R,C,Color), Color != 6.

% 0 inside region bounded by 6 (but not by 2) => 2
:- input(R,C,0), interior_6(R,C), not interior_2(R,C),
   output(R,C,Color), Color != 2.

% 0 inside region bounded by 2 => 2
:- input(R,C,0), interior_2(R,C), output(R,C,Color), Color != 2.
</asp>

<attempt_5>
<asp>
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  Grid size
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  4‑connected moves
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
delta(0, 1). delta(0, -1). delta(1, 0). delta(-1, 0).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  Border cells (the outer edge of the grid)
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
border(R, C) :- R = 0.
border(R, C) :- R = N-1.
border(R, C) :- C = 0.
border(R, C) :- C = M-1.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  Flood‑fill for wall colour 3
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
outside_3(R, C) :- border(R, C), input(R, C, V), V != 3.
outside_3(R2, C2) :- outside_3(R1, C1), delta(DR, DC),
                     R2 = R1 + DR, C2 = C1 + DC,
                     input(R2, C2, V), V != 3.

% Flood‑fill for wall colour 4
outside_4(R, C) :- border(R, C), input(R, C, V), V != 4.
outside_4(R2, C2) :- outside_4(R1, C1), delta(DR, DC),
                     R2 = R1 + DR, C2 = C1 + DC,
                     input(R2, C2, V), V != 4.

% Flood‑fill for wall colour 6
outside_6(R, C) :- border(R, C), input(R, C, V), V != 6.
outside_6(R2, C2) :- outside_6(R1, C1), delta(DR, DC),
                     R2 = R1 + DR, C2 = C1 + DC,
                     input(R2, C2, V), V != 6.

% Flood‑fill for wall colour 2
outside_2(R, C) :- border(R, C), input(R, C, V), V != 2.
outside_2(R2, C2) :- outside_2(R1, C1), delta(DR, DC),
                     R2 = R1 + DR, C2 = C1 + DC,
                     input(R2, C2, V), V != 2.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  Determine the colour for zero cells.
%  Hierarchy: 3 → 4 → 6 → 2 → 8 → 0
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
output(R, C, 4) :-
    input(R, C, 0),
    not outside_3(R, C).

output(R, C, 6) :-
    input(R, C, 0),
    outside_3(R, C),
    not outside_4(R, C).

output(R, C, 2) :-
    input(R, C, 0),
    outside_3(R, C),
    outside_4(R, C),
    not outside_6(R, C).

output(R, C, 8) :-
    input(R, C, 0),
    outside_3(R, C),
    outside_4(R, C),
    outside_6(R, C),
    not outside_2(R, C).

output(R, C, 0) :-
    input(R, C, 0),
    outside_3(R, C),
    outside_4(R, C),
    outside_6(R, C),
    outside_2(R, C).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  Non‑zero cells keep their original colour
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
output(R, C, V) :-
    input(R, C, V),
    V != 0.

% The above definitions force exactly one colour per cell, 
% producing a unique answer set that matches the expected output.
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
</asp>

n_cols(M) :- M = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

                    input(R2,C2,V), V != 4.

% Flood‑fill for wall colour 6

```
% Grid dimensions
max_row(Rmax) :- Rmax = #max{R : input(R, _, _)}.
max_col(Cmax) :- Cmax = #max{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% 4‑connected moves
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Border cells
border(R,C) :- R = 0.
border(R,C) :- R = Rmax.
border(R,C) :- C = 0.
border(R,C) :- C = Cmax.

% Flood‑fill for wall colour 3
outside_3(R,C) :- border(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 3.
outside_3(R2,C2) :- outside_3(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC), R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
                    input(R2,C2,V), V != 3.

% Flood‑fill for wall colour 6
outside_6(R,C) :- border(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 6.
outside_6(R2,C2) :- outside_6(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC), R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
                    input(R2,C2,V), V != 6.

% Flood‑fill for wall colour 8
outside_8(R,C) :- border(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 8.
outside_8(R2,C2) :- outside_8(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC), R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
                    input(R2,C2,V), V != 8.

% Output generation
% non‑zero cells keep their original colour
output(R,C,V) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != 0.

% zeros that are reachable from the border (outside the outermost wall 3)
output(R,C,0) :-
    input(R,C,0), outside_3(R,C).

% zeros inside the wall 3 but outside the inner wall 6 become colour 6
output(R,C,6) :-
    input(R,C,0), not outside_3(R,C), outside_6(R,C).

% zeros inside the walls 3 and 6 but outside the innermost wall 8 become colour 8
output(R,C,8) :-
    input(R,C,0), not outside_3(R,C), not outside_6(R,C), outside_8(R,C).

% zeros inside all three walls stay 0
output(R,C,0) :-
    input(R,C,0), not outside_3(R,C), not outside_6(R,C), not outside_8(R,C).

% Exactly one colour per cell
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(C) } 1 :- input(R,C,_).
```

<transformation>
The input is treated as a tile map. The output is NxM times larger than the input (where the input is N rows x M cols). Each input cell at (BR, BC) with a non-zero value causes a copy of the full input to be placed in the corresponding NxM block of the output (at rows BR*N .. BR*N+N-1, cols BC*M .. BC*M+M-1). Each input cell with value 0 produces an all-zero block at that position.
</transformation>

<asp>
% Count distinct row/col indices — equals grid dimensions for 0-indexed inputs
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(N) :- N = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Output is NR*NR rows and NC*NC cols (input tiled by itself)
output_cell(R, C) :-
    input(BR, BC, _), input(DR, DC, _),
    n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
    R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC.

1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Non-zero block: the sub-cell must equal the input value at the relative position
:- input(BR, BC, V), V != 0,
   input(DR, DC, TileColor),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != TileColor.

% Zero block: every sub-cell must be 0
:- input(BR, BC, 0),
   input(DR, DC, _),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != 0.
</asp>
</example_1>

<asp>
% Output has the same dimensions as the input
output_cell(R, C) :- input(R, C, _).
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Grid boundary coordinates
min_row(R) :- R = #min{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
max_row(R) :- R = #max{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
min_col(C) :- C = #min{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.
max_col(C) :- C = #max{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.

% 4-connected adjacency offsets
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Non-3 cells on the grid boundary are reachable from outside
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_row(Rmin), R = Rmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_row(Rmax), R = Rmax.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_col(Cmin), C = Cmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_col(Cmax), C = Cmax.

% Flood-fill outward through non-3 cells
outside(R2, C2) :-
    outside(R1, C1), delta(DR, DC),
    R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
    input(R2, C2, V), V != 3.

% Color-3 cells stay 3
:- input(R, C, 3), output(R, C, Color), Color != 3.

% Outside non-3 cells stay 0
:- outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 0.

% Interior non-3 cells (enclosed by 3s, not reachable from boundary) become 4
:- input(R, C, 0), not outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 4.
</asp>

<asp>
% Dimensions
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Wall colours – colours that appear on the outer border and are not 0
wall_color(W) :- input(R,C,W), (R = 0; R = N-1; C = 0; C = M-1), W != 0.

% Cells whose colour is not a wall colour
non_wall(W) :- not wall_color(W).

% Start cells for the flood‑fill: boundary cells whose colour is not a wall colour
boundary_nonwall(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), not wall_color(V), R = 0; R = N-1; C = 0; C = M-1.

% Cells reachable from a non‑wall boundary cell without crossing a wall
outside(R,C) :- boundary_nonwall(R,C).
outside(R2,C2) :- outside(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC), R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
                input(R2,C2,V), non_wall(V).

% One predicate for each cell (so the cardinality can enforce a unique colour)
cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% Exactly one output colour for every cell
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- cell(R,C).

% Enforce the correct colour according to the rule
% – cells that are “outside” must keep their original colour
:- output(R,C,Color), input(R,C,V), outside(R,C), Color != V.

% – interior zeros become 4
:- output(R,C,Color), not outside(R,C), input(R,C,V), V = 0, Color != 4.

% – interior non‑zero cells keep their colour
:- output(R,C,Color), not outside(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 0, Color != V.

% 4‑connected adjacency
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).
</asp>

<asp>
% Determine grid dimensions
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Walls: any colour that appears on the outer border (row 0,
% row N‑1, column 0, column M‑1) and is not 0.
% The colour is stored in a unary predicate wall_color/1.
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
wall_color(Wc) :- input(R, C, Wc), R = 0,   Wc != 0.
wall_color(Wc) :- input(R, C, Wc), R = N-1, Wc != 0.
wall_color(Wc) :- input(R, C, Wc), C = 0,   Wc != 0.
wall_color(Wc) :- input(R, C, Wc), C = M-1, Wc != 0.

% Colours that are not walls
non_wall(Wc) :- not wall_color(Wc).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Start of the flood‑fill: boundary cells whose colour is not a wall.
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
boundary_nonwall(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), not wall_color(V), R = 0.
boundary_nonwall(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), not wall_color(V), R = N-1.
boundary_nonwall(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), not wall_color(V), C = 0.
boundary_nonwall(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), not wall_color(V), C = M-1.

% 4‑connected moves
delta(0, 1). delta(0, -1). delta(1, 0). delta(-1, 0).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Flood‑fill: cells reachable from a non‑wall boundary cell without
% crossing a wall colour.
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
outside(R, C) :- boundary_nonwall(R, C).
outside(R2, C2) :- outside(R1, C1), delta(DR, DC),
                R2 = R1 + DR, C2 = C1 + DC,
                input(R2, C2, V), non_wall(V).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Helper predicates
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
cell(R, C) :- input(R, C, _).

% Exactly one output colour per cell
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(C) } 1 :- cell(R, C).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% 1. Cells that are reachable from the boundary keep their original colour.
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
:- outside(R, C), input(R, C, V), output(R, C, Color), Color != V.

% 2. Interior non‑zero cells (not reachable) keep their original colour.
:- not outside(R, C), input(R, C, V), V != 0,
   output(R, C, Color), Color != V.

% 3. Interior zero cells become colour 4.
interior_zero(R, C) :- not outside(R, C), input(R, C, 0).
:- interior_zero(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 4.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% The transformation is now fully encoded.
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
</asp>

% ---------- dimensions ----------
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% ---------- 4‑connected moves ----------
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% ---------- border cells ----------
border(R,C) :- R = 0.
border(R,C) :- R = N-1.
border(R,C) :- C = 0.
border(R,C) :- C = M-1.

% ---------- wall 3 ----------
outside_3(R,C) :- border(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 3.
outside_3(R2,C2) :- outside_3(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC), R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
                    input(R2,C2,V), V != 3.
interior_3(R,C) :- not outside_3(R,C).

% ---------- wall 4 ----------
outside_4(R,C) :- border(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 4.
outside_4(R2,C2) :- outside_4(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC), R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
                    input(R2,C2,V), V != 4.
interior_4(R,C) :- not outside_4(R,C).

% ---------- wall 6 ----------
outside_6(R,C) :- border(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 6.
outside_6(R2,C2) :- outside_6(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC), R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
                    input(R2,C2,V), V != 6.
interior_6(R,C) :- not outside_6(R,C).

% ---------- wall 2 ----------
outside_2(R,C) :- border(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 2.
outside_2(R2,C2) :- outside_2(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC), R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
                    input(R2,C2,V), V != 2.
interior_2(R,C) :- not outside_2(R,C).

% ---------- exactly one output colour per cell ----------
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(C) } 1 :- input(R,C,_).

% ---------- non‑zero cells keep their colour ----------
:- input(R,C,V), V != 0, output(R,C,Color), Color != V.

% ---------- interior zeros become the next colour ----------
% 0 inside region bounded by 3 (but not by 4) => 4
:- input(R,C,0), interior_3(R,C), not interior_4(R,C),
   output(R,C,Color), Color != 4.

% 0 inside region bounded by 4 (but not by 6) => 6
:- input(R,C,0), interior_4(R,C), not interior_6(R,C),
   output(R,C,Color), Color != 6.

% 0 inside region bounded by 6 (but not by 2) => 2
:- input(R,C,0), interior_6(R,C), not interior_2(R,C),
   output(R,C,Color), Color != 2.

% 0 inside region bounded by 2 => 2
:- input(R,C,0), interior_2(R,C), output(R,C,Color), Color != 2.

<asp>
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  Grid size
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  4‑connected moves
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
delta(0, 1). delta(0, -1). delta(1, 0). delta(-1, 0).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  Border cells (the outer edge of the grid)
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
border(R, C) :- R = 0.
border(R, C) :- R = N-1.
border(R, C) :- C = 0.
border(R, C) :- C = M-1.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  Flood‑fill for wall colour 3
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
outside_3(R, C) :- border(R, C), input(R, C, V), V != 3.
outside_3(R2, C2) :- outside_3(R1, C1), delta(DR, DC),
                     R2 = R1 + DR, C2 = C1 + DC,
                     input(R2, C2, V), V != 3.

% Flood‑fill for wall colour 4
outside_4(R, C) :- border(R, C), input(R, C, V), V != 4.
outside_4(R2, C2) :- outside_4(R1, C1), delta(DR, DC),
                     R2 = R1 + DR, C2 = C1 + DC,
                     input(R2, C2, V), V != 4.

% Flood‑fill for wall colour 6
outside_6(R, C) :- border(R, C), input(R, C, V), V != 6.
outside_6(R2, C2) :- outside_6(R1, C1), delta(DR, DC),
                     R2 = R1 + DR, C2 = C1 + DC,
                     input(R2, C2, V), V != 6.

% Flood‑fill for wall colour 2
outside_2(R, C) :- border(R, C), input(R, C, V), V != 2.
outside_2(R2, C2) :- outside_2(R1, C1), delta(DR, DC),
                     R2 = R1 + DR, C2 = C1 + DC,
                     input(R2, C2, V), V != 2.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  Determine the colour for zero cells.
%  Hierarchy: 3 → 4 → 6 → 2 → 8 → 0
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
output(R, C, 4) :-
    input(R, C, 0),
    not outside_3(R, C).

output(R, C, 6) :-
    input(R, C, 0),
    outside_3(R, C),
    not outside_4(R, C).

output(R, C, 2) :-
    input(R, C, 0),
    outside_3(R, C),
    outside_4(R, C),
    not outside_6(R, C).

output(R, C, 8) :-
    input(R, C, 0),
    outside_3(R, C),
    outside_4(R, C),
    outside_6(R, C),
    not outside_2(R, C).

output(R, C, 0) :-
    input(R, C, 0),
    outside_3(R, C),
    outside_4(R, C),
    outside_6(R, C),
    outside_2(R, C).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  Non‑zero cells keep their original colour
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
output(R, C, V) :-
    input(R, C, V),
    V != 0.

% The above definitions force exactly one colour per cell, 
% producing a unique answer set that matches the expected output.
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
</asp>

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Determine the grid dimensions – we need the outermost row and column.
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
max_row(Rmax) :- Rmax = #max{R : input(R, _, _)}.
max_col(Cmax) :- Cmax = #max{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% 4‑connected moves
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
delta(0, 1). delta(0, -1). delta(1, 0). delta(-1, 0).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Border cells – the outer edge of the grid
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
border(R, C) :- R = 0.
border(R, C) :- R = Rmax.
border(R, C) :- C = 0.
border(R, C) :- C = Cmax.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Flood‑fill for each wall colour (3, 6 and 8).
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
outside_3(R, C) :- border(R, C), input(R, C, V), V != 3.
outside_3(R2, C2) :- outside_3(R1, C1), delta(DR, DC),
                     R2 = R1 + DR, C2 = C1 + DC,
                     input(R2, C2, V), V != 3.

outside_6(R, C) :- border(R, C), input(R, C, V), V != 6.
outside_6(R2, C2) :- outside_6(R1, C1), delta(DR, DC),
                     R2 = R1 + DR, C2 = C1 + DC,
                     input(R2, C2, V), V != 6.

outside_8(R, C) :- border(R, C), input(R, C, V), V != 8.
outside_8(R2, C2) :- outside_8(R1, C1), delta(DR, DC),
                     R2 = R1 + DR, C2 = C1 + DC,
                     input(R2, C2, V), V != 8.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Output definition
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% 1. Non‑zero cells keep their original colour
output(R, C, V) :-
    input(R, C, V), V != 0.

% 2. Zero cells that are reachable from the border (outside the outermost wall 3)
output(R, C, 0) :-
    input(R, C, 0), outside_3(R, C).

% 3. Zero cells inside the wall 3 but not inside the inner wall 6 become colour 6
output(R, C, 6) :-
    input(R, C, 0), not outside_3(R, C), outside_6(R, C).

% 4. Zero cells inside both walls 3 and 6 but not inside the innermost wall 8 become colour 8
output(R, C, 8) :-
    input(R, C, 0), not outside_3(R, C), not outside_6(R, C), outside_8(R, C).

% 5. Zero cells inside all three walls stay 0
output(R, C, 0) :-
    input(R, C, 0), not outside_3(R, C), not outside_6(R, C), not outside_8(R, C).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Exactly one colour per cell – enforced by cardinality constraint
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(C) } 1 :- input(R, C, _).
</asp>
